title: `podman machine ssh` not doing the expected on Windows/WSL
body:
When using podman machine I would expect `podman machine ssh` to handle the 'current' or initial/default machine. Instead it uses the default podman system connection?


```powershell
PS> .\podman.exe system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity                                     Default
crc                          ssh://core@127.0.0.1:2222/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock   C:\Users\gbraad\.crc\machines\crc\id_ecdsa   true
podman-machine-default       ssh://user@localhost:59971/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  C:\Users\gbraad\.ssh\podman-machine-default  false
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@localhost:59971/run/podman/podman.sock            C:\Users\gbraad\.ssh\podman-machine-default  false

PS> .\podman.exe machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:59971' (ECDSA) to the list of known hosts.
core@localhost's password:

PS> .\podman.exe system connection remove crc
PS> .\podman.exe machine ssh
Error: no service destination configured
```
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1894/183009051-dbb03357-e53a-48af-b8f0-bc17f324c1f4.png)

**Current results**
It uses the default system connection

**Expected results**
It should not use the default system connection and use this one as the endpoint for `machine ssh`.
Instead it should use the active podman-machine-default

This might be a Windows specific issue (atm not able to verify this on Linux or macOS)

comments.body:
```go
func remoteConnectionUsername() (string, error) {
   ...

   dest, _, err := cfg.ActiveDestination()
```

returns a `"no service destination configured"`.

On Linux verified by @praveenkumar to do the following:
```
$ podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                          Identity                                   Default
crc                          ssh://core@127.0.0.1:2222/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock   /home/prkumar/.crc/machines/crc/id_ecdsa   true
crc-root                     ssh://core@127.0.0.1:2222/run/podman/podman.sock             /home/prkumar/.crc/machines/crc/id_ecdsa   false
podman-machine-default       ssh://core@localhost:42403/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  /home/prkumar/.ssh/podman-machine-default  false
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@localhost:42403/run/podman/podman.sock            /home/prkumar/.ssh/podman-machine-default  false
```

```
$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Warning: Permanently added '[localhost]:42403' (ED25519) to the list of known hosts.
Fedora CoreOS 36.20220723.2.2
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos

Last login: Fri Aug  5 11:48:08 2022 from 192.168.127.1
[core@localhost ~]$ 
```

on macos verified by @anjannath:
```
$ which podman
/opt/podman/bin/podman

$ podman system connection list
Name                         URI                                                         Identity                                  Default
crc                          ssh://core@127.0.0.1:2222/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock  /Users/anjan/.crc/machines/crc/id_ecdsa   true
crc-root                     ssh://core@127.0.0.1:2222/run/podman/podman.sock            /Users/anjan/.crc/machines/crc/id_ecdsa   false
podman-machine-default       ssh://core@localhost:51017/run/user/501/podman/podman.sock  /Users/anjan/.ssh/podman-machine-default  false
podman-machine-default-root  ssh://root@localhost:51017/run/podman/podman.sock           /Users/anjan/.ssh/podman-machine-default  false

$ podman machine list
NAME                    VM TYPE     CREATED       LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE
podman-machine-default  qemu        16 hours ago  Currently running  1           2.147GB     107.4GB

$ podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
Fedora CoreOS 36.20220723.2.2
Tracker: https://github.com/coreos/fedora-coreos-tracker
Discuss: https://discussion.fedoraproject.org/tag/coreos
```

which clearly shows the behaviour is different.

# podman machine ssh
Connecting to vm podman-machine-default. To close connection, use `~.` or `exit`
ssh: connect to host localhost port 51057: Connection refused
Error: exit status 255

############
有两种情况能发生上述类似的问题：
1、主机系统启动初期，通过 Windows 服务执行 msys bash 脚本文件，在脚本文件中执行 podman machine start，并创建几个文件，用于将主机系统的运行环境报告给 WSL，命令如：powershell -command "wsl.exe -d podman-machine-default enterns touch /var/run/systemd-detect/virtualization-no" 2>/dev/null
然而这么做之后，在重启主机系统后，就可能出现 ssh 连接问题
2、Linux 系统下（Linux Mint20），通过 VirtualBox 启动 win10.vhd 虚拟硬盘，此时运行于虚拟环境中的系统，在执行 podman machine start 后，会提示 ：请启用虚拟机平台 Windows 功能并确保在 BIOS 中启用虚拟化
这时，关闭 win10 虚拟系统，关闭 Linux 主机系统，再直接启动 win10 系统，结果就会出现 ssh 连接问题

出现的这种问题，找不到解决办法，只能重新安装 podman machine，然后再避免上述的情况
1、Windows 服务依赖于 "主机网络服务"，且等待 ssh 能够正常执行时，执行类似命令：cmd /C "podman machine ssh $_machineName sudo touch $_respath/virtualization-no" 2>/dev/null
2、虚拟环境中的系统避免启动 podman machine
@pdj9na has filed a new issue instead: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/15209 as this was off-topic to the original issue.
@gbraad Unfortunately I don't have a windows box ATM to verify, but looks like there are some comments on your PR.

@ashley-cui One comment from @Luap99 which addresses this 'looks wrong' as the code should never return nil when there is no error. In other words, you agree `validVM` should be `true` after load succeeded without an error?

There is a definitely a problem in this code; behaviour is different between the providers.

```go
	vm, err = provider.LoadVMByName(vmName)
```

`loadByVMName` didn't return `nil` but a valid value. So this is no error returned. Originally I had:

```go
  vm, err = provider.LoadVMByName(vmName)
  if (err == nil) {  // vm != nil
    validVM = true;
  }
```

as I believe this is what the result of the test should be, as this state isn't set or otherwise used after the load. Wouldn't the load return a 'valid' VM if no error followed? Somehow the Linux implementation regards this as a valid 'remote connection' when validVM is not modified? Is this something you can verify?

I can't verify on Linux/macOS myself now, so I can't compare the differences (on PTO and my remote machine is unreachable now).
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Has any of the work done by @cdoern fix this?  Any update on this?
Have to take a look. Haven't been able to run this recently.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
We will assume the issue is resolved. If it isn't, please add a new comment and we will reopen.
